title: Crash on OOM: unchecked `PyTuple_New` / `PyDict_New` in C `PartialCallableObjectProxy.__call__`
author: GrahamDumpleton
body:
## Summary

The C implementation of `PartialCallableObjectProxy.__call__`
(`WraptPartialCallableObjectProxy_call` in `src/wrapt/_wrappers.c`) does not
check the return values of two allocation calls:

1. `fnargs = PyTuple_New(...)` — used to build the combined positional
   argument tuple from the partial's stored args and the call-site args.
2. `fnkwargs = PyDict_New()` — used to build the combined keyword argument
   dict.

If either allocation fails (e.g. under low-memory conditions), the function
returns `NULL` with `MemoryError` set, but the C code proceeds to use the
`NULL` pointer:

- After a failed `PyTuple_New`, the following loop calls
  `PyTuple_SetItem(NULL, i, item)`, which dereferences a `NULL` pointer
  and crashes the interpreter.
- After a failed `PyDict_New`, the subsequent `PyDict_Update(NULL, ...)`
  calls similarly dereference `NULL`, and the existing error path would
  also `Py_DECREF(NULL)`.

## Credits

Thanks to @devdanzin for the report via:

* https://gist.github.com/devdanzin/e1ac0c43dc4d4278ee82455da4859872
